title: 10 best herbal tinctures for acid reflux
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for acid reflux are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are taken orally to alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. These natural remedies address the root cause of acid reflux by promoting digestive health and reducing inflammation. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with acid reflux include marshmallow root, chamomile, licorice root, and slippery elm. These herbs soothe the esophageal lining, reduce stomach acid production, and improve overall gut function. By incorporating herbal tinctures into their routine, individuals can find relief from acid reflux symptoms, enhancing their quality of life and enabling them to enjoy meals without discomfort or pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which are often irritated by acid reflux. The tincture can also help to produce mucus, which can protect the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to have a soothing effect on the gastrointestinal system, which can help to reduce the symptoms of acid reflux, such as heartburn and regurgitation. Furthermore, aloe vera tincture can help to regulate gastric acid secretion, preventing the excessive production of acid that can cause acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera tincture reduces inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like bradykinase, salicylic acid, and various polysaccharides.

Mucoprotective: The mucoprotective properties of aloe vera tincture protect and soothe the mucous membranes of the esophagus, reducing irritation caused by acid reflux, thanks to the gel's complex carbohydrate structure and glycoprotein content.
Antisecretory: Aloe vera tincture inhibits gastric acid secretion, helping to prevent acid reflux, as a result of its anthraquinones and aloin content.

Antioxidant: Aloe vera tincture neutralizes free radicals and promotes wound healing, which is beneficial for acid reflux-induced damage to the esophageal lining, due to its high vitamin C and E content, as well as various carotenoids and flavonoids.
Prokinetic: Aloe vera tincture stimulates gastrointestinal motility, facilitating digestion and preventing acid reflux, because of its anthraquinones, particularly aloin and anthracene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant contain a gel-like substance that is commonly used to soothe acid reflux symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory and mucilaginous properties.
Stems: The stems of the aloe vera plant contain a juice that can be used to make medicinal tea for acid reflux, as it helps to reduce inflammation and promote digestion.
Flowers: The flowers of the aloe vera plant contain nectar that can be used to make a soothing tea for acid reflux, as it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the aloe vera plant contain a gel-like substance that can be used to make medicinal tea for acid reflux, as it helps to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 large aloe vera leaves, carefully removing the green outer skin to expose the inner gel.
Chop the gel into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of your chosen menstruum (50/50 vodka and water, or pure apple cider vinegar) in a clean jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any remaining liquid and discarding the spent plant material.
Store the finished tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it refrigerated for longest shelf life, and take 1-2 teaspoons as needed for acid reflux relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains a substance called mucilage, which can form a protective barrier over the mucous membranes lining the stomach and esophagus. This barrier can help prevent stomach acid from backing up into the esophagus, thereby reducing the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, marshmallow tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating discomfort and promoting healing. The demulcent effect of marshmallow tincture can also help increase the mucus production in the gastrointestinal tract, providing additional protection against stomach acid.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tincture creates a protective film on the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux. This soothing effect is primarily due to the high mucilage content in marshmallow root.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tincture help alleviate inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow root contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects.

Mucilaginous: The mucilaginous nature of marshmallow tincture helps to lubricate and soften the tissues in the gastrointestinal tract, promoting healing and reducing the discomfort associated with acid reflux. This property is attributed to the high polysaccharide content in marshmallow root.

Astringent: Marshmallow tincture has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone tissues in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Tannins found in marshmallow root are responsible for its astringent effects.

Vulnerary: Marshmallow tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration in the gastrointestinal tract, aiding in the recovery from acid reflux damage. The flavonoids and polysaccharides in marshmallow root contribute to its vulnerary properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe acid reflux symptoms.
Roots: Contain phenolic acids, which can help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter and prevent acid reflux.
Seeds: High in mucilage, which can form a protective layer in the stomach and esophagus, reducing acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried marshmallow root into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean jar.
Securely seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
Take 1-2 mL of the marshmallow root tincture up to three times a day as needed for acid reflux relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains compounds that can increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. It also helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, licorice tincture has been shown to help balance stomach acid levels, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux occurring. This herbal remedy can provide natural relief for those suffering from acid reflux, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice tincture forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, protecting them from irritation and damage caused by acid reflux. This property is important for acid reflux because it helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the esophagus. The active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides, such as glycyrrhizin, present in the licorice root.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. This property is important for acid reflux because it helps to minimize the damage caused by stomach acid, promoting healing and providing relief. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and triterpenoids, including glycyrrhizin and glabridin.

Mucilage production: Licorice tincture stimulates the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. This property is important for acid reflux because it helps to prevent direct contact between stomach acid and the esophageal lining, reducing the risk of damage and irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides, such as glycyrrhizin, present in the licorice root.

Gastric mucosal protection: Licorice tincture helps to strengthen and maintain the integrity of the gastric mucosal lining, preventing the erosion caused by stomach acid. This property is important for acid reflux because it helps to reduce the risk of developing ulcers and other complications associated with acid reflux. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and triterpenoids, including glycyrrhizin and glabridin.

Reducing gastric acid secretion: Licorice tincture has been shown to inhibit the production of gastric acid, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. This property is important for acid reflux because it helps to maintain a healthy pH balance in the stomach, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux episodes. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and triterpenoids, including glycyrrhizin and glabridin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tincture plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tincture plant contain flavonoids that can help reduce stomach acid production and provide relief from acid reflux.
Rhizomes: Licorice rhizomes contain compounds that can help heal and soothe the esophageal lining, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the licorice root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the solids and keeping the liquid.
Store the finished licorice tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for acid reflux relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains a powerful antioxidant called silymarin, which can help strengthen and repair the damaged mucous membranes of the esophagus caused by acid reflux. Moreover, milk thistle has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, milk thistle can help improve digestion and reduce gastric acid production, further reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Overall, milk thistle tincture can provide a natural and effective way to manage acid reflux and promote digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle tincture can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which is important for acid reflux as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and irritation. This property is due to the active constituent, silymarin.

Antioxidant: Milk thistle tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by acid reflux, thus promoting healing. The active constituent responsible for this property is also silymarin.

Mucilage: Milk thistle tincture contains mucilage, a substance that can form a protective coating on the mucous membranes, providing a soothing effect and reducing discomfort associated with acid reflux.
Nervine: Milk thistle tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress-induced acid reflux. This property is attributed to the flavonoids present in milk thistle.

Cholagogue: Milk thistle tincture can stimulate the production of bile, which aids in digestion and can help prevent acid reflux caused by poor digestion. This property is due to the active constituent, silymarin.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid that helps strengthen the liver and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Leaves: Milk thistle leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce acid reflux.
Flowers: Milk thistle flowers contain antioxidants that can help protect the digestive system from damage caused by acid reflux.
Rhyzomes: Milk thistle rhyzomes have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including acid reflux, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried milk thistle seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the seeds until fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and take 1-2 dropperfuls up to three times a day before meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tincture helps with acid reflux because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the digestive system, including the esophagus, which can be irritated by acid reflux. Additionally, dandelion has been shown to support healthy digestion and promote the production of digestive enzymes, which can help the body break down food more efficiently and reduce the likelihood of acid reflux. Furthermore, dandelion is a natural diuretic, which can help the body eliminate excess fluids and reduce the pressure on the stomach, thereby reducing the risk of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones like taraxacin, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.

Digestive stimulant: The bitter principles in dandelion tincture, such as taraxacin and taraxacerin, promote the production of digestive enzymes and bile, aiding in proper digestion and reducing acid reflux.
Mucilaginous: Dandelion tincture's mucilaginous properties, from polysaccharides like inulin, create a protective coating on the gastric mucosa, preventing acid reflux-induced damage and irritation.

Diuretic: The diuretic effect of dandelion tincture, attributed to potassium and tannins, helps eliminate excess fluid and toxins from the body, reducing pressure on the digestive system and alleviating acid reflux.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties, due to phenolic acids like chlorogenic acid, help protect cells from oxidative stress and support overall gastrointestinal health, contributing to the management of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelion have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including acid reflux, due to their ability to support healthy digestion and soothe the stomach lining.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation, making them useful for managing acid reflux symptoms and promoting overall gut health.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers contain bioactive compounds that may help regulate stomach acid production, providing relief for acid reflux and promoting a healthy gastrointestinal system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh dandelion leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Chop the dandelion leaves and flowers, then place them in a clean glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the dandelions, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Take 1-2 dropperfuls (20-40 drops) of the tincture up to three times a day for acid reflux relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with acid reflux because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help soothe and heal the irritated esophageal lining caused by acid reflux. Its bitter properties can also stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, improving overall digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to have antispasmodic effects on the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which can help relieve the symptoms of acid reflux such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent properties help to tighten and tone the tissues of the esophagus, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. This is due to the tannins present in yarrow.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids such as luteolin and apigenin.

Antispasmodic: Yarrow tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, preventing spasms that can lead to acid reflux. This is due to the presence of lactones like costunolide and dehydrocostus lactone.

Digestive stimulant: Yarrow tincture can help to improve digestion and reduce the production of stomach acid, thereby reducing acid reflux. This is due to the bitter principles like a-pinene and sabinene in yarrow.

Mucilaginous: Yarrow tincture can form a protective layer over the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, providing a barrier against stomach acid. This is due to the polysaccharides present in yarrow.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, helping reduce gastric acid production and providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Stems: Have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing acid reflux episodes.
Seeds: Contain potent volatile oils that can stimulate digestion, alleviate symptoms of acid reflux, and improve overall gastrointestinal health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully dried and free of moisture.
Chop the dried yarrow into small pieces and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over the chopped yarrow, ensuring the herb is completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer, discarding the spent herb, and store the tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for acid reflux.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract. It may also help to relax the muscles in the esophagus, allowing for the proper flow of food and digestive juices, and preventing the backflow of stomach acid. Additionally, skullcap tincture has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger acid reflux. Overall, skullcap tincture can provide natural relief for acid reflux symptoms and support overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Skullcap tincture can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids such as scutellarin.

Antispasmodic: It can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating spasms and pain associated with acid reflux, thanks to the active constituents like catalpol and verbascoside.

Nervine: Skullcap tincture can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms, by acting on the central nervous system and promoting relaxation, due to its content of volatile oils and bitter iridoids.

Anxiolytic: Its anxiolytic properties can help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep, which can contribute to improved gastrointestinal function and reduced acid reflux, due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Skullcap tincture can help protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative stress, reducing the risk of acid reflux and promoting overall digestive health, due to its rich antioxidant content, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of tannins that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe the digestive system and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and mucilage that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce acid production.
Stems: Have anti-spasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Roots: Contain alkaloids that can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce the symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried skullcap herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system. These compounds help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, the muscle that controls the opening between the esophagus and stomach. When this muscle is relaxed, it prevents stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, thereby reducing the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, valerian tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe irritation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux. Overall, valerian tincture can provide natural relief for acid reflux symptoms without the need for harsh pharmaceutical treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Valerian tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. This property is due to the presence of antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Spasmolytic: Valerian tincture can relax the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating spasms and preventing acid reflux. This is attributed to the plant's alkaloids, particularly valerenic acid.
Anxiolytic: Valerian tincture has anxiety-reducing properties, which can help decrease stress-induced acid reflux. This is primarily due to the plant's valepotriates and valerenic acid content.

Sedative: Valerian tincture can promote sleep and improve sleep quality, which may help reduce instances of nighttime acid reflux. This effect is attributed to the valepotriates and valerenic acid content.

Antacid: Valerian tincture has a mild antacid effect, which can help neutralize stomach acid and provide short-term relief from acid reflux symptoms. This is due to the presence of tannins and mucilage in the plant.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are commonly used in making medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their natural sedative properties that can help relax the esophageal muscles and reduce symptoms.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are rich in antispasmodic compounds, making them an ideal part of the plant for alleviating acid reflux symptoms by reducing gastrointestinal spasms.
Flowers: Valerian flowers contain volatile oils and other beneficial compounds that can help soothe the digestive system, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.

Seeds: Valerian seeds are a lesser-known but still effective part of the plant for treating acid reflux. They contain essential oils that can help regulate gastrointestinal motility and reduce acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried valerian root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the chopped valerian root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and take 1-2 dropperfuls up to three times a day as needed for acid reflux relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains passiflora incarnata, a plant that has been used in traditional medicine to treat gastrointestinal issues. It is believed to work by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, maypop tincture may help to relax the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. This dual action of reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles makes maypop tincture a potentially effective natural remedy for acid reflux. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maypop tincture can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms, due to the presence of alkaloids such as sedridine and chrysin.
Spasmolytic: It helps to relax the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating spasms and discomfort associated with acid reflux, thanks to the active constituent scutellarin.
Antacid: Maypop tincture can neutralize stomach acid, offering quick relief from acid reflux symptoms, due to its mildly basic nature.
Antioxidant: It can protect the lining of the esophagus from oxidative damage caused by acid reflux, thanks to the presence of various flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Anxiolytic: Maypop tincture can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms, due to its content of passionflower alkaloids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop tincture plant contain isoquinoline alkaloids, which can help reduce gastric acid secretion and relieve symptoms of acid reflux.
Leaves: The leaves of the maypop tincture plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritation in the esophagus and reduce acid reflux symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop tincture plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to help reduce gastric acid production and provide relief from acid reflux.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop tincture plant are rich in mucilage, which can help protect the lining of the stomach and esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ripe maypop (Passiflora incarnata) fruits, ensuring they easily detach from the vine.
Rinse the maypop fruits, then separate the pulp from the seeds and peel, discarding the inedible parts.
Combine 1 cup of prepared maypop pulp with 2 cups of 80-100 proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar, ensuring the pulp is fully submerged.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid; store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it refrigerated for up to 1 year. Take 1-2 ml up to three times a day as needed for acid reflux.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tincture helps with acid reflux because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Ginger has been shown to increase gastric motility, which can help move food through the digestive system more efficiently, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Additionally, ginger has been found to help relax the lower esophageal sphincter, which can prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, further reducing the symptoms of acid reflux. Overall, ginger tincture can be a natural and effective way to help manage acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tincture can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and irritation. This property is due to the presence of gingerols and shogaols.

Prokinetic: Ginger tincture can help speed up the movement of food through the digestive tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. This property is due to the presence of 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.
Antiemetic: Ginger tincture can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of acid reflux. This property is due to the presence of gingerols and shogaols.

Antioxidant: Ginger tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the digestive tract from damage, which is important for acid reflux as it can help reduce inflammation and irritation. This property is due to the presence of gingerols, shogaols, and paradols.

Carminative: Ginger tincture can help relieve gas and bloating, which are common symptoms of acid reflux. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as zingiberene and beta-sesquiphellandrene.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory and digestive properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract.
Leaves: Ginger leaves can also be used in tea to treat acid reflux, as they contain phenolic compounds that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce the production of stomach acid.

Flowers: Ginger flowers are sometimes used in tea for acid reflux due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.

Seeds: Ginger seeds are not commonly used in tea for acid reflux, but they do contain compounds that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce inflammation, making them potentially useful for treating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh ginger root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80 proof or higher vodka over the ginger, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the ginger pieces.
Label the bottle with the ingredients and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for acid reflux relief.
